top_stories.title: Okta extends AI agent security to Amazon Bedrock, opens platform to rival identity providers
top_stories.summary: Okta expanded its Okta for AI Agents platform to support any agent ecosystem, enterprise resource, and identity provider, including a new integration with Amazon Bedrock. The platform extension notably opens Okta's AI agent security capabilities to rival identity providers, broadening its interoperability scope significantly.

top_stories.title: Tycoon2FA hijacks Microsoft 365 accounts via device-code phishing
top_stories.summary: The Tycoon2FA phishing kit has been updated to support device-code phishing attacks targeting Microsoft 365 accounts. The kit abuses Trustifi click-tracking URLs as part of its attack chain to bypass authentication controls and hijack accounts.

top_stories.title: Google lets Workspace admins apply one policy across all SAML apps
top_stories.summary: Google has updated Context-Aware Access (CAA) in Google Workspace to support a default policy assignment that automatically applies to all SAML-based applications without an existing specific policy. This creates a universal security baseline for SSO-enabled third-party and internal applications using Google Workspace credentials.

top_stories.title: Akamai to acquire LayerX for $205 million
top_stories.summary: Akamai has entered a definitive agreement to acquire browser security vendor LayerX for $205 million. The acquisition is intended to extend Akamai's zero trust security portfolio with browser-based AI usage controls and secure enterprise browser technology.

top_stories.title: The Next Cybersecurity Challenge May Be Verifying AI Agents
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the emerging cybersecurity challenge of verifying and managing the identities of AI agents. It covers the need for trusted identity standards, verification mechanisms, and runtime controls as AI agents become more prevalent in enterprise environments.

news.title: Inside the REMUS Infostealer: Session Theft, MaaS, and Rapid Evolution
news.summary: The REMUS infostealer malware focuses on stealing browser sessions and authentication tokens rather than traditional passwords, operating as a Malware-as-a-Service (MaaS) platform. The analysis covers how session theft bypasses MFA and authentication controls, directly undermining identity and access management protections.

news.title: Cisco patches another actively exploited SD-WAN zero-day (CVE-2026-20182)
news.summary: Cisco has patched an actively exploited authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-20182) in its Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and SD-WAN Manager, attributed to a highly sophisticated threat actor. The flaw stems from a flawed peering authentication mechanism and affects both on-premises and cloud deployments.

news.title: CISA Adds Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20182 to KEV After Admin Access Exploits
news.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-20182, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies are required to remediate the issue by May 17, 2026.

news.title: Palo Alto Networks Idira: A next-gen platform for human, agentic & machine identity security
news.summary: Palo Alto Networks has unveiled Idira, a new trademarked next-generation identity security platform targeting human, agentic, and machine identity security. The platform is positioned around AI-era identity challenges, extending beyond traditional IAM to address emerging agentic and non-human identity use cases.

news.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
news.summary: Cisco has released patches for a maximum-severity authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-20182, CVSS 10.0) in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and SD-WAN Manager that is being actively exploited. The flaw affects peering authentication mechanisms, allowing attackers to gain administrative access without valid credentials.

news.title: Regional routing for AWS access portals: Implementing custom vanity domains for IAM Identity Center
news.summary: AWS has launched multi-Region replication for IAM Identity Center, enabling customers to implement regional routing and custom vanity domains for their access portals. The feature allows workforce users to access a single portal for AWS accounts and applications with improved redundancy and regional distribution.

news.title: Identity breaches are on the rise, and are only getting worse - so how can your business stay safe?
news.summary: CyberArk has issued a warning about the growing risk of identity breaches, highlighting AI agents' access to sensitive files as an emerging threat vector. The company advocates for a unified, automated identity security approach to address the increasing frequency and severity of identity-related incidents.

news.title: Interview: AI optimism and upskilling for a shifting job market
news.summary: Okta's chief marketing officer Shannon Duffy shared views on AI's potential as a productivity booster and job market equalizer in an interview. The discussion focuses on workforce upskilling and AI optimism rather than Okta's IAM products or services.

news.title: Most Organizations Now Use AI Agents for Sensitive Security Tasks
news.summary: A Semperis study found that most organizations are now deploying AI agents for sensitive security tasks, with 74% believing AI will increase attacks on identity infrastructure. The research highlights growing concern among IAM and security professionals about AI-driven threats targeting identity systems.

news.title: Microsoft Patches 138 Vulnerabilities, Including DNS and Netlogon RCE Flaws
news.summary: Microsoft released patches for 138 security vulnerabilities, including 30 rated Critical, covering flaws in DNS, Netlogon, and other products. Among the 138 flaws, 61 are privilege escalation vulnerabilities, which are directly relevant to access control and identity security.

news.title: New Semperis Study Reveals AI's Effects on the Identity Attack Surface
news.summary: Semperis released a study examining how AI agents are expanding the identity attack surface, particularly around Active Directory, Entra ID, and Okta. The research found that organizations are granting AI agents access to critical systems faster than they are implementing identity security safeguards.

news.title: Keeper Security Delivers Structured Governance for Humans and AI Agents with New PAM Approval Workflows in KeeperPAM
news.summary: Keeper Security has launched new PAM Approval Workflows within its KeeperPAM platform, introducing structured governance and approval-based controls for privileged access. The new Keeper Workflow capability is designed to provide administrators with oversight of privileged access for both human users and AI agents.

news.title: Worm Redux: Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Infections Bite Supply Chain
news.summary: Hundreds of npm packages in the TanStack open source ecosystem have been infected by a self-propagating, credential-stealing worm attributed to TeamPCP. The worm spreads through the supply chain and is designed to steal credentials from affected systems.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted involvement in SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign collected authentication tokens without deploying malware, enabling silent credential theft at scale.
